title: Trade Routes
description: Major travel corridors that shaped movement through the biblical lands.

features.name: Via Maris
features.description: The coastal route connecting Egypt, the Levant, and the northern powers.
features.caution: The line is schematic and follows an approximate corridor rather than one fixed road.

features.name: King's Highway
features.description: A highland route east of the Jordan used for trade and long-distance travel.
features.caution: The road is reconstructed from geography and ancient travel patterns.

features.name: Ridge Route / Way of the Patriarchs
features.description: The central ridge route linking southern and northern hill country settlements.
features.caution: This corridor is a study reconstruction rather than a single preserved road.
